perturbation.original_text: Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person, or any entity with which the party has a business relationship, regardless of control
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by broadening the definition of 'Affiliate' to include any entity with a business relationship, regardless of control. This contradicts the subsequent use of 'Affiliate' in section 1.3 which speaks of subsidiaries and other Affiliates and implies a level of control not merely a business relationship. This uncertainty impacts enforcement, because section 1.3 makes the covenants, agreements, and obligations extend to all Affiliates, with conflicting definitions it is unclear to which entities the terms apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bach of the parties hereto agree that all of their covenants, agreements and obligations hereunder shall extend to and be binding upon and may be enforced against any and all of their respective subsidiaries and other Affiliates, as well as against the parties themselves, as the case may be, and that the names of Glamis and Western Copper will herein be deemed to refer collectively to Glamis and all of its subsidiaries and other Affiliates and to Western Copper and all of its subsidiaries and other Affiliates, respectively.
perturbation.justification: The change broadens the definition of 'Affiliate' from entities under common control to any entity with a business relationship, regardless of control. This directly contradicts the contradicted section, which implies a level of control when referring to 'subsidiaries and other Affiliates'. The original text restricts 'Affiliates' to entities with control, while the changed text includes entities without control. Since the contradicted text uses the term 'Affiliates' in the context of obligations and enforcement, the expanded definition creates uncertainty and a strong contradiction regarding which entities these obligations extend to, making enforcement ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the ' Disposition Notice") of the intended disposition. For a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.changed_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the ' Disposition Notice") of the intended disposition. For a period of 15 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 15 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the initial negotiation period for Right of First Offer from 30 days to 15 days, while keeping the 30 day period for when Glamis may dispose of the property, creating ambiguity about which of those two deadlines is relevant. This change affects how section 2.2 is interpreted, creating an ambiguity. It is unclear which time frame is correct, resulting in uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the ' Disposition Notice") of the intended disposition. For a period of 15 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 15 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the initial negotiation period from 30 days to 15 days while retaining a 30-day period in a subsequent clause ('If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period'). This creates an inconsistency regarding the negotiation timeframe and when Glamis is free to dispose of the property. The contradicted text, which is the same section, contains both time periods, making it unclear whether the negotiation period is 15 or 30 days, thus triggering the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because different sections of the contract provide conflicting deadlines. This ambiguity creates uncertainty and potential disputes, as it is unclear which timeframe governs the negotiation period. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Glamis' Right of TerminaHon 3.6 Glamis may, at its option, terminate this Agreement by written notice to Western Copper, effective immediately upon delivery of the notice, should Western Copper cease conducting business in the normal course, become insolvent, make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, suffer or permit the appointment of a receiver for its business or assets or avail itself of, or become subject to, any proceedings under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act (Canada) or any other statute of any province or state relating to insolvency or the protection of rights of creditors.
perturbation.changed_text: Glamis' Right of TerminaHon 3.6 Glamis may, at its option, terminate this Agreement by written notice to Western Copper, effective 30 days after delivery of the notice, should Western Copper cease conducting business in the normal course, become insolvent, make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, suffer or permit the appointment of a receiver for its business or assets or avail itself of, or become subject to, any proceedings under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act (Canada) or any other statute of any province or state relating to insolvency or the protection of rights of creditors.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction in sections 3.5 and 3.6. Section 3.5 states that Western Copper can terminate the agreement effective immediately upon delivery of written notice to Glamis. However, section 3.6 now states that Glamis can terminate the agreement effective 30 days after delivery of written notice to Western Copper. This difference in termination effective dates creates uncertainty and ambiguity about which term should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Western Copper's Right of Termination 3.5 W'estem Copper may, at its option, terminate this Agreement by written notice to Glamis, effective immediately upon delivery of the notice, should Glamis cease conducting business in the normal course, become insolvent, make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, suffer or permit the appointment of a receiver for its business or assets or avail itself of, or become subject to, any proceedings under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act (Canada) or any other statute of any province or state relating to insolvency or the protection of rights of creditors.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Glamis can terminate the agreement effective immediately upon delivery of the notice. The changed text modifies this to state that Glamis can terminate the agreement effective 30 days after delivery of the notice. This directly contradicts section 3.5, which states that Western Copper can terminate the agreement effective immediately. The difference in the effective termination dates creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, thus creating a contradiction.
